fields.description:
## Context

When practitioners triage agent-generated bug tickets, they encounter free-form, inconsistent descriptions that slow diagnosis. Titles lack consistent structure for searchability, descriptions omit expected vs actual behavior, and raw logs are either missing or pasted without context. The existing ticket CLI accepts any description, which is correct — blocking bug creation with strict format enforcement would cause agents to skip reporting entirely. By providing a template with post-creation validation warnings (never gates), a lightweight create-bug skill, and using-dso guidance, agents get three chances to produce high-quality reports without any barrier to tracking issues.

## Success Criteria

- A shared bug report template exists at plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/bug-report-template.md with required sections (Expected Behavior, Actual Behavior) and optional sections (Technical Environment, Action History and Workarounds, Logs, Additional Details)
- The Logs section includes guidance to truncate at 30K characters and use ticket comments for overflow
- The template specifies a structured title format: [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] with the component hierarchy (Tool/Skill -> File/Path -> Logical Workflow)
- The ticket CLI auto-converts unicode arrow (U+2192) to ASCII -> in titles on create and edit, eliminating unicode compatibility issues with Jira
- Post-creation validation prints a warning to stderr (exit code 0) when the title does not match the [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] pattern, directing the agent to edit the title via the ticket edit command
- Post-creation validation prints a separate warning to stderr (exit code 0) when the description is missing Expected Behavior or Actual Behavior headers (case-insensitive check), directing the agent to edit the description via the ticket edit command
- Post-creation validation prints a warning to stderr (exit code 0) when the description exceeds 30K characters, directing the agent to move excess content to ticket comments
- Bugs are always created regardless of format compliance — warnings never block creation and exit code is always 0
- The title pattern warning is configurable via dso-config.conf (key: bug_report.title_warning_enabled, default: true) so projects with different title conventions can opt out
- A lightweight /dso:create-bug skill exists that references the template and the correct ticket command; the skill does not add --tags CLI_user by default (only the calling agent knows whether the user explicitly requested the bug). This is a reference skill (guidance doc), not a behavioral skill — no eval scaffold required.
- Using-dso guidance (in the project using-dso doc, to be created by epic dso-khl2 or added inline if not yet available) instructs agents to use the template when creating bugs
- An integration test creates a ticket with a non-conforming title and a description missing required headers, and asserts that warnings are emitted to stderr, exit code is 0, and the ticket is persisted — validating the end-to-end create-and-warn flow
- A smoke test invokes the /dso:create-bug skill with a realistic bug scenario and asserts the generated ticket description contains Expected Behavior and Actual Behavior headers — validates the guidance layer produces template-conformant output
- An actionable remediation test creates a ticket without required headers, captures stderr warning text, and asserts the warning includes (a) the ticket edit command syntax, (b) the specific missing header names, and (c) the path to the bug report template — confirming warnings provide complete remediation guidance

## Dependencies

- No hard blockers. The create-bug skill is a lightweight reference doc (wraps a ticket command with template guidance), not a behavioral skill requiring eval scaffolding.
- Soft ordering: using-dso doc (epic dso-khl2) should ideally exist before the using-dso guidance SC can be completed; if not yet available, the guidance can be added as an inline note in the skill itself until the doc is created.
- Post-creation validation is added inline to the ticket CLI create command output path — no new hook surface or extensibility is required.

## Approach

Three-layer guidance with post-creation validation. A shared template (bug-report-template.md) defines the format with the Zero Inference rule (no root cause speculation, no hindsight bias, no forced debugging, no low-value tickets). Post-creation validation in the ticket CLI warns on missing title pattern, missing description headers, and oversized descriptions — warnings only to stderr, never blocking, exit code always 0. Unicode arrow auto-converted to ASCII on create/edit. Title warning configurable via dso-config.conf. A lightweight create-bug skill references the template. Using-dso guidance provides the third reminder.

## Scenario Analysis

- 30K log truncation advisory only — oversized tickets corrupt branch state (high, runtime): Addressed by adding a description size warning at 30K.
- Post-creation validation not covered by tests (high, deployment): Addressed by TDD requirement and integration/smoke/remediation tests (SC 12-14).
- Stderr warnings swallowed by $(...) pattern (medium, runtime): Accepted risk — the other two layers (skill + using-dso) still apply.
- Shim enforcement blocks create-bug skill (medium, deployment): Addressed by using .claude/scripts/dso shim.
- Title pattern not configurable (medium, config): Addressed by adding dso-config.conf key.
- Create-bug skill may bypass CLI_user tag logic (medium, config): Addressed by not adding CLI_user by default.
- Unicode lookalike arrows trigger false positives (low, runtime): Addressed by auto-converting unicode arrow to ASCII on create/edit.

## Research Findings

Discovery session (captured in epic comments) established: structured title format using Component-Behavior pattern, 0-4 priority rubric tied to blocker/workaround criteria, Zero Inference rule prohibiting root cause speculation and hindsight bias, chronological rationalization as a black-box recorder, scenario-specific rules for user-flagged/sub-agent/nitpick bugs, and gating criteria for low-value ticket prevention. Web research confirmed Jira supports UTF-8 in summary fields when database is configured for UTF-8 (default for Jira Cloud), but misconfigured instances may mangle unicode — use ASCII -> as canonical format.

## Fidelity Review

Agent Clarity: self_contained 4, success_measurable 5. Scope: right_sized 4, no_overlap 5, dependency_aware 4. Value: user_impact 3 (reviewer wants quantified baseline — data not available; pain point acknowledged as credible), validation_signal 4.
